title: This Week in Finance & Tech
body:
It has been a busy week across the global business and technology landscape. Here is a quick look at the stories moving the needle.

Technology and AI

There is a mix of concern and optimism in the tech sector. Micron shares are rising, which analysts say points to renewed investor confidence in the semiconductor market. Meanwhile, in a sign of how valuable AI hardware has become, Singapore authorities have seized a $42 million mansion as part of an investigation into the illegal smuggling of servers containing AI chips.

Automation is also hitting the headlines. In Australia, dock workers are asking for a 28-hour work week as they navigate the impact of AI testing at major ports. Elsewhere, China is looking to leverage its existing EV supply chain dominance to gain a foothold in the global robotaxi market.

Travel and Infrastructure

Travelers heading to Europe this summer might face some disruption. Ryanair is urging governments to delay the new EU border system, warning that it could cause significant queues and chaos at airports.

In Australia, a major outage at the country's largest telecoms company caused widespread issues, affecting train services and emergency call lines. While the problem was traced to servers in Sydney and Melbourne, the underlying cause is still being investigated.

Policy and Markets

In the U.S., interest rates remain a hot topic. Donald Trump has criticized the Federal Reserve again, noting that he plans to remove governor Lisa Cook from the board. He also commented on the role of Kevin Warsh, suggesting he will have to make his own decisions regarding interest rate policy.

Finally, for gaming fans, the release details for Grand Theft Auto VI are now clear. The game is set to launch on November 19, 2026, for PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series S/X consoles.
cta: You can keep up with the full picture every day at HEADLINNE.com.
